description: Unit: `transform.compose_text` is registered in the runtime action registry with Transform-category metadata.
Unit: compose-text input schema accepts multiple outputs with document payloads and rejects missing `outputs` arrays.
Unit: stable keys are generated from freeform labels using a reference-safe format.
Unit: stable-key generation disambiguates collisions when two labels normalize to the same reference-safe token.
Unit: editing an output label preserves the existing stable key by default.
Unit: empty output labels are rejected by compose-text authoring validation.
Unit: duplicate output labels in the same step are rejected by compose-text authoring validation.
Unit: duplicate stable keys in the same step are rejected by compose-text validation.
Unit: compose-text rejects template document nodes that fall outside the supported markdown-safe block and inline subset.
Unit: template document validation accepts simple reference nodes and rejects non-simple expression-like paths.
Unit: BlockNote-to-template serialization preserves literal text, inline references, and supported markdown-safe formatting without emitting BlockNote node shapes.
Unit: template-to-BlockNote hydration restores inline reference placeholders and supported markdown-safe formatting for authoring round-trip.
Unit: the constrained editor schema omits media and other unsupported content node types.
Unit: markdown rendering emits paragraphs and hard breaks correctly from the template document model.
Unit: markdown rendering emits bullet and ordered list structure correctly from supported template blocks.
Unit: markdown rendering emits supported inline marks such as bold, italic, code, and links correctly around literal text.
Unit: runtime composition resolves simple payload references into markdown output text.
Unit: runtime composition resolves prior-step output references into markdown output text.
Unit: runtime composition fails when a referenced payload value is missing and includes output-key context.
Unit: runtime composition fails when a referenced prior-step output value is missing and includes reference-path context.
Unit: compose-text returns a plain object of markdown strings keyed by stable keys when multiple outputs succeed.
Unit: static output schema for compose-text accepts string-valued object results without requiring a fixed compile-time field list.
Unit: dynamic output schema resolution for compose-text produces an object schema with one string property per configured stable key.
Unit: dynamic output schema resolution for compose-text updates when outputs are added, removed, or renamed while stable keys remain unchanged.
Unit: workflow data-context building surfaces compose-text outputs under `vars.<saveAs>.<stableKey>` with string field types.
Unit: workflow reference browsing labels compose-text outputs using the author-facing label while navigating through the stable key path.
Component: the dedicated compose-text editor renders an output list and allows selecting among multiple outputs.
Component: authors can add a new composed output and receive a generated stable key from the initial label.
Component: authors can reorder composed outputs without changing the stable key of an existing output.
Component: the editor shows the stable key and a copyable downstream reference path for each output.
Component: inserting a reference from the workflow source browser creates an inline reference chip in the constrained editor.
Component: deleting an inline reference chip removes the corresponding reference node from the serialized template document.
Component: unsupported media insertion affordances are absent from the compose-text editor UI.
Component: duplicate labels produce inline validation feedback in the compose-text editor.
Component: duplicate stable keys produce inline validation feedback in the compose-text editor.
Component: invalid manual stable-key edits or explicit key regenerations are surfaced clearly to the author if that control is exposed.
Integration: publish-time workflow validation uses the config-derived compose-text output schema rather than a generic object when validating downstream references.
Integration: an AI prompt field can consume `vars.<saveAs>.<stableKey>` from a compose-text step through ordinary reference mode with no prompt-specific wiring.
Integration: an email or notification text field can consume `vars.<saveAs>.<stableKey>` from a compose-text step through ordinary reference mode with no consumer-specific wiring.
Integration: compose-text step execution succeeds end to end with multiple outputs, mixed literal content, markdown-safe formatting, and inline references.
Integration: compose-text step execution fails the entire step rather than partially succeeding when one output contains a missing reference.
Regression: existing transform actions continue to register, validate, and expose downstream output fields unchanged after compose-text is added.
Regression: authoring and runtime behavior for `ai.infer` remain unchanged when no compose-text step is present.
Regression: persisted compose-text step config does not contain BlockNote node-type artifacts or media payloads after editor round-trip.
